summary:
• Strait of Hormuz disruption risks are creating volatility in Western Hemisphere crude markets, with geopolitical tensions between the U.S., Iran, and Iraq's new government potentially threatening global supply routes and pushing traders to reassess regional stability.

• Canada's oil sector faces dual pressures from trade war uncertainties and domestic policy shifts, while U.S. crude inventories are declining despite gasoline stock buildups, signaling potential demand weakness in the Americas' largest market.

• Depleted U.S. oil and gas workforce levels since 2022 are constraining production capacity expansion precisely when geopolitical risks to Middle Eastern and global supplies are elevated, limiting Americas' ability to offset international supply disruptions.
